quoniam spērāvī in tē. ^ Preserve me: quoniam bonōrum meōrum nōn egēs.~»^ I have said to the Lord
for thou hast no need of my goods."

1 ^ Conservā mē: 3 ^ Sanctīs quī sunt in terrā ejus
Domine: mīrificāvit omnēs voluntātēs meās in eīs. ^ To the saints who are in his land
quoniam spērāvī in tē. ^ Preserve me: he hath made wonderful all my desires in them.

1 ^ Conservā mē: 4 ^ Multiplicātae sunt infirmitātēs eōrum~; posteā accelerāvērunt. Nōn congregābō conventicula eōrum dē sanguinibus
Domine: nec memor erō nōminum eōrum per labia mea. ^ Their infirmities were multiplied~; afterwards they made haste. I will not gather together their meetings for bloodofferings
quoniam spērāvī in tē. ^ Preserve me: nor will I be mindful of their names by my lips.

Domine: et calicis meī~; tū es quī restituēs hērēditātem meam mihī. ^ The Lord is the portion of my inheritance and of my cup~; it is thou that wilt restore my inheritance to me.

1 ^ Conservā mē: 6 ^ Fūnēs cecidērunt mihī in praeclārīs~; etenim hērēditās mea praeclāra est mihī. ^ The lines are fallen unto me in goodly places~; for my inheritance is excellent to me.

7 ^ Benedīcem Dominum quī tribūvit mihī intellectum~; insuper et usque ad noctem increpuērunt mē rēnēs meī. ^ I will bless the Lord who hath given me understanding~; moreover also my reins have corrected me even till night.
8 ^ Prōvidēbam Dominum in conspectū meō semper
quoniam spērāvī in tē. ^ Preserve me: nē commovear. ^ I set the Lord always in my sight
O Lord: for he is at my right hand

1 ^ Conservā mē: 9 ^ Propter hoc laetātum est cor meum
Domine: et exsultāvit lingua mea~; insuper et carō mea requiēscet in spē. ^ On account of this my heart hath been glad
quoniam spērāvī in tē. ^ Preserve me: and my tongue hath rejoiced~; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope.

1 ^ Conservā mē: 10 ^ Quoniam nōn dērelinquēs animam meam in infernō
Domine: nec dabis sanctum tuum vidēre corruptiōnem. ^ Because thou wilt not leave my soul in hell
quoniam spērāvī in tē. ^ Preserve me: nor wilt thou give thy holy one to see corruption.

1 ^ Conservā mē: 11 ^ Notās mihī fēcistī viās vītae~; adimplēbis mē laetitiā cum vultū tuō~; dēlectātiōnēs in dexterā tuā usque in fīnem. ^ Thou hast made known to me the ways of life
Domine: thou shalt fill me with joy with thy countenance~; at thy right hand are delights even to the end.
